About: It is about an abstract strategy board game for two players.
Affects: It affects the opponent's position to set up a devastating counterattack.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Christian Freeling.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the draughts (or checkers) family of games.
CausedBy: It is caused by the need for connected vacant squares to achieve more complicated multicapture moves.
ConsistsOf: It consists of 18 pieces per player.
CreatedBy: It was created by Christian Freeling.
CreatedTo: It was created to incorporate capture by approach into a draughts framework.
HasA: It has linear movement of men.
It has a distinctive trapezoid shape for piece arrangement.
It has a game tree complexity estimated to be around 10^107.
It has an upper bound state-space complexity of ~10^40.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by majority capture.
It is characterized by ladder and bridge tactics.
It is characterized by the coup turc.
It is characterized by the oblique hit.
It is characterized by the king's trap.
It is characterized by linear movement and capture.
It is characterized by forced capture.
It is characterized by the removal of captured men at the end of the turn.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the game draughts (or checkers).
It was influenced by the game Fanorona.
It was influenced by the game Bushka.
It was influenced by the game Croda.
InventedBy: It was invented by Christian Freeling.
IsA: It is an abstract strategy board game for two players.
It is a variant of the game draughts (or checkers).
It is played on an 8×8 checkered gameboard.
It is a checkers variant with the smallest percentage of draws.
KnownFor: It is known for its unique tactics and strategies.
It is known for having very few draws.
OriginatedIn: It originated in Christian Freeling's 1988 game Bushka.
PublishedAs: It was published as a variant of the game draughts (or checkers).
PublishedIn: It was published in Abstract Games.
UsedFor: It is used for competitive play.
It is used for online tournaments.
UsedIn: It is used in a draughts framework.
UsedTo: It is used to manipulate the opponent's position.
It is used to set up a devastating counterattack.
It is used to achieve more complicated multicapture moves.
It is used to speed up the game.
It is used to allow for quicker entry into the mid and late game.
It is used to apply linear movement to a draughts game.
It is used to win by moving c3 to e5.
It is used to capture men on c4 and d3.
It is used to capture black men on d5 and c6.
It is used to defend by taking advantage of linear movement.
